steps.title: Use the point on the circle
steps.content:
Since $(12,c)$ lies on the circle, substitute $x=12$ and $y=c$ into the circle equation:
$$(x-5)^2+(y+3)^2=49$$
$$(12-5)^2+(c+3)^2=49$$

steps.title: Find the constant
steps.content:
The only number with square $0$ is $0$, so:
$$c+3=0$$
The answer is -3.
